steps.text: Inject lixiviant underground to pass through the ore zone and mobilize the uranium.
Pump the uranium-bearing solution to the surface via extraction wells.
Process the solution to extract the uranium.

Keep the other Party generally informed of its relevant plans and schedules, respond to the other Party's requests for information to the extent reasonable and practical, and strive to recognize and minimize any problems arising from each Party's regulatory activities in implementing this MOU.
Make staff members of the Parties readily available for collaboration, information sharing, and resolution of each Party's concerns.
Work in good faith to resolve concerns in a timely manner and avoid undue delays that could adversely impact each Party's ability to carry out its statutory responsibilities under AEA §§ 84 and 275.

Treat the drafts of any proposed or final rules, including any preambles (statements of consideration) thereof, and any other related documents or information, in whatever form, that are transmitted or otherwise communicated between the Parties as pre-decisional and deliberative information to the extent permitted under Freedom

Request the concurrence of the EPA in writing from the Director, Office of Nuclear Material Safety and Safeguards, to the EPA through the Director, Office of Radiation and Indoor Air, including a copy of the NRC's draft final rule.
Make all reasonable efforts to resolve any disagreement concerning the EPA's concurrence collegially and quickly.
Raise the disagreement to the Office of the Executive Director for Operations for the NRC and to the Assistant Administrator for Air and Radiation for the EPA.
Resolve all disagreements prior to publication of the final rule in the Federal Register.
Designate a representative and alternate representative to ensure coordination between the EPA and the NRC.
Provide written notice to the other Party to change the designated representative.
Extend or amend the MOU through mutual written agreement of the Parties.
Provide written notice to the other Party to terminate participation in the MOU.
Execute the MOU by signing on the dates shown.

preconditions.text: Should the EPA choose to promulgate standards of general application under its authority in AEA § 275
NRC rulemaking would promulgate general requirements for the management of byproduct material.
A disagreement arises between the Parties concerning the EPA's concurrence regarding the NRC's draft final rule.
Initial collegial efforts fail to resolve the disagreement.
The MOU must be signed by the authorized officials of the Parties to become effective.
Written notice of termination must be provided at least 30 days in advance of the desired termination date.

constraints.text: Without proper controls, altered groundwater can potentially migrate from the production zone to groundwater outside of the ISR wellfield.

Neither Party will disclose or transmit the draft regulations or other related documents or information to any third party unless expressly agreed to by the other Party or as required by applicable law.
This MOU is not intended to be legally binding and does not alter, limit, or supersede statutory authorities.
All commitments are subject to the availability of appropriated funds and budget priorities.
Each Party retains all immunities and defenses provided by law with respect to any action based on or occurring as a result of this MOU.
This MOU does not create any right or benefit, substantive or procedural, enforceable by law or equity, by persons who are not party to this agreement.
